seoTitle: How to Save an Image From Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Download an image embedded in a Google Doc using the Save to Keep shortcut or a Web Page export, keeping full resolution and original file format.
intro: Extracting a high-resolution image from inside a Google Doc isn't always obvious since there's no direct download button on the picture itself. This guide covers two reliable methods: using the Save to Keep shortcut for a quick single-image download, and exporting the whole document as a Web Page to pull every embedded image at once. Both preserve the original image quality.

steps.heading: Select your target image
steps.body: Right-click the graphic, logo, or photo inside your document to bring up the image options context menu.

steps.heading: Use Save to Keep for a quick export
steps.body: Choose Save to Keep to instantly clone the image into your Google Keep notepad sidebar.

steps.heading: Download from the Keep panel
steps.body: Open the Keep side panel, right-click the saved image, and download it directly to your local computer or storage.

steps.heading: Or export the whole document as a Web Page
steps.body: Go to File, then Download, and choose Web Page (.html, zipped) to export your entire document along with all embedded images.

steps.heading: Unzip and access the images folder
steps.body: Extract the downloaded zip file to find a dedicated images folder containing every embedded graphic at full resolution.

faq.question: Will saving an image this way reduce its quality?
faq.answer: No, both the Save to Keep method and the Web Page export preserve the original image resolution and file format.

faq.question: What file format will my extracted image be in?
faq.answer: Images typically retain their original format, such as PNG or JPEG, when extracted through either method.

faq.question: Is there a faster way if I need every image from a long document?
faq.answer: Yes, exporting the whole document as a zipped Web Page is faster than saving each image individually through Google Keep.

faq.question: Do I need the Google Keep app installed to use the Save to Keep shortcut?
faq.answer: No, Google Keep works through the side panel directly inside Google Docs, so no separate app installation is required.
